topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring intrusion on a quiet Tuesday afternoon. My breath hitched in my chest as I saw the name: Mark. Mark, the architect of scraped knees and whispered cruelties in the third grade. A wave of coldness washed over me, settling deep in my stomach. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally clicked on the request. His profile picture was the same smug grin I remembered from years ago, mocking and sharp. A sudden tightness gripped my throat, making it hard to swallow. I reread the empty space where a message should be, the silence of my room amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears. What did he want? The urge to delete the request was overwhelming, a desperate need to erase this unwelcome tether to a painful past.

[story 2]

I stared at my phone, the screen illuminating the faint shadows under my eyes. It was an unexpected ping. Mark. The name felt like a physical blow, knocking the air out of my lungs. My hands started to sweat, slick against the cool glass of the device. Every muscle in my body seemed to tense up, preparing for an impact I couldn't see. *Why now?* The thought echoed relentlessly in my mind, a frantic loop of old insecurities surfacing. I took a slow, shaky breath, trying to anchor myself to the solid reality of the wooden floor beneath my feet. A strange, hollow ache bloomed behind my ribs, a feeling of being exposed, as if someone had just pried open a carefully sealed box.

[story 3]

When I opened the app, the sight of Mark’s profile picture made my vision swim momentarily. My heart began to hammer against my ribs with an alarming speed, a frantic rhythm against my sternum. I felt a distinct urge to throw the phone across the room, to shatter the glass and escape the suffocating feeling that had suddenly descended upon me. My palms were damp, and I instinctively curled my arms around my torso, seeking a small measure of physical protection. A low, shaky sound escaped my lips when I finally managed to type a single, hesitant question into the message box, the words tasting like ash on my tongue.
